summary: PR #731 re-integrated the `tools/calcprogress` subtree into repository formatting/newline checks by removing calcprogress from exclusion filters, then normalized whitespace and executable mode inside the calcprogress files so those checks would pass. The PR body only says "Same as #727," so intent beyond fixing the calcprogress subtree is inferred from the diff.
what_changed: Removed `tools/calcprogress` from the EditorConfig workflow exclusion regex, leaving only `tools/m2c` excluded from changed-file filtering.
Removed `tools/calcprogress` from `tools/newlines.sh` exclusions, leaving only `tools/m2c` excluded from newline normalization.
Changed `tools/calcprogress/calcprogress.py` file mode from `100644` to executable `100755`.
smart_moves: Fixed the subtree by first making calcprogress compliant with repository whitespace expectations, then removing the CI/script exclusions that had treated it as special.
Kept `tools/m2c` excluded while narrowing the exception, avoiding a broader tooling policy change than necessary.
Used small formatting-only edits in calcprogress files, minimizing risk to the progress calculation logic.
decomp_lessons: Vendored or subtree-style tooling should not remain permanently exempt from repository hygiene checks once it can be made compliant.
Before removing CI exclusions, normalize the target subtree's whitespace and newline issues in the same PR to avoid breaking checks.
Mode bits matter for repo tooling: scripts intended to be run directly, such as `calcprogress.py`, may need executable permissions tracked in git.
naming_conventions: The project refers to the progress tool as `calcprogress` under `tools/calcprogress`.
The workflow and shell filters use path-based exclusions for tool subtrees, e.g. `tools/m2c`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No decompilation matching tactics were changed in this PR.

The README text preserved the existing calcprogress design note: progress is calculated from sizes of remaining assembly files and `.section` directives rather than by assuming decompiled status from the map.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR body only stated "Same as #727," so any reviewer rationale or relationship to #727 is not available from this slice.
follow_up_queries: Look up PR #727 to understand the referenced prior fix and whether the same subtree-exclusion pattern was corrected elsewhere.
Search for remaining `tools/calcprogress` exclusions in CI or scripts to confirm the subtree is fully covered by repository hygiene checks.
Check whether `calcprogress.py` is invoked directly anywhere, validating the executable bit change.
